column7: I use Workday Financial Management for processing payroll, and it helps me simplify payroll processing. With the centralized and automated system, payroll becomes more accurate and easier to manage. I like the interface because it makes performing day-to-day tasks easy, and the system is quite user-friendly compared to traditional tools. The navigation becomes smooth once you get used to it. I enjoy how it makes handling payroll-related activities more efficient, giving an overall modern and streamlined experience. The reporting capability is another feature I really like; it's easy to generate reports and get real-time data, which is very helpful during payroll processing and reconciliation. Having everything centralized means I don’t have to switch between multiple systems, making the workflow much smoother and more efficient.
column8: One thing that could be improved is the learning curve. For new users, it can take some time to fully understand where everything is and how different processes work. Also, certain tasks require multiple steps or clicks, which can feel a bit time-consuming at times. Sometimes the system can be slightly slow when loading reports or large data sets. Improving navigation for specific functions and making some processes more streamlined would make the overall experience even better.
column10: I find Workday Financial Management centralizes and automates payroll, reducing errors and manual work. It offers real-time reporting and simplifies reconciliation, saving time and effort. The user-friendly interface streamlines payroll tasks, making my workflow more efficient and modern. Effortless Payroll Management with

column7: What I love the most about Workday Financial Management is automating daily accounting expanses, making it easier to build and share reports. I mainly use it for company related expanses, and it really helps keeping things organized and with clear boundaries.
column8: In general, it’s very positive, but I’d say that it’s interface is not very user friendly, which makes it hard to use and find things sometimes. Related to this, the usability is not very intuitive, but that won’t keep you from using it, it’ll just make it a little harder.
column10: I mainly use it for expenses, reports and reimbursements, and it helps me by automating the reports and info recognition, so I don’t have to manually type the expanses or fill in all details in the report. Automates Expenses and Makes

column7: I had used Workday Financial Management to manage the full accounts payable cycle, including processing invoices, approvals, vendor management, payment tracking, and reporting. I also monitored team productivity, resolved invoice discrepancies, and generated reports in real time. The thing I liked the most is its user-friendly interface and the visibility it provides into the entire accounts payable process. For me, the ability to access financial data quickly and generate reports on demand has been extremely useful. The interface makes it easier for both new and experienced team members to navigate the system with minimal training. The real-time reporting is especially valuable because it gives immediate visibility into AP activity and financial data without waiting for manual updates or separate report generation. I can quickly identify bottlenecks, monitor outstanding invoices, and track team workload to support month-end close activities more effectively. Additionally, it's a strong financial management platform with excellent workflow automation, reporting capabilities, and visibility into financial operations.
column8: One challenge is that certain workflow can feel overly complex, especially when handling exception or non-standard invoices. Also the reporting function is powerful, but creating highly customized report can have a learning curve and may require additional training for users who are not familiar with the system.
column10: I use Workday Financial Management to manage the full accounts payable cycle, automate invoice routing, and track payments. It reduces manual data entry, provides visibility into approvals, and prevents payment delays. The user-friendly interface aids team navigation with minimal training. Streamlined AP Workflows with

column7: I like using Workday Financial Management for streamlining the procurement process, as it means we don't have to handle requests through emails or documents directly. Instead, the use of formal channels in Workday simplifies the process. The UI is a big plus for me as it's much more modern compared to what we used before. I also appreciate the suggested prefilled fields and the configuration options available, allowing different users to see only the relevant fields they need. This customization really helps in reducing complexity, especially for those who aren't directly working in procurement. The guide feature is also quite useful, assisting people in filling out the necessary details like cost codes and centers. Overall, it's a more straightforward and modern tool fitting well with our other software solutions.
column8: There could be more flexibility to have more custom fields. In our business, it all gets quite complicated, but we have to fit into a relatively rigid system. The fields you can customize are relatively constrained, and a bit more flexibility would be helpful.
column10: I use Workday Financial Management to streamline the procurement process, avoiding direct emails or documents. It makes requisition recording and requests more formal and organized. Modern UI Simplifies Procurement, Needs More

column7: I really appreciate the drill-down capability in Workday Financial Management. It's a huge time-saver because I can click through a high-level reconciliation balance directly to individual invoices or journal lines without having to jump between different systems. It streamlines the Record to Report workflow and keeps audit trails centralized and cloud-based. Workday eliminates the nightmare of version control and manual data matching that spreadsheets bring during period-end close. It's a single source of truth that standardizes the reconciliation process, handles high-volume transactions automatically, and attaches audit trails directly to journal entries. I also find the advanced, real-time reporting features and automated journal creation very useful. Plus, as a cloud-native platform, it's fast and accessible no matter where the team is working.
column8: The interface can feel a bit rigid sometimes, especially when you are first learning where everything lives. Setting up custom automated reconciliation rules or pulling very specific, non-standard reports can be a bit clunky and usually requires some trial and error to get the logic exactly right. It’s a powerful tool if you handle heavy reconciliation volumes, but make sure your team invests in proper training upfront to understand the data logic, otherwise, navigation can feel overwhelming at first. The initial setup was a pretty heavy lift. From an RTR perspective, the biggest challenge was the data mapping phase. Setting up the automated reconciliation rules and getting the matching logic exactly right also required a lot of trial and error. There was definitely a steep learning curve for the team initially, and it required a lot of change management and training.
column10: Workday Financial Management eliminates the nightmare of version control and manual data matching during period-end close by acting as a single source of truth. It speeds up timelines and reduces audit stress with automatic transaction handling and attached audit trails. Comprehensive and Robust for Large

column7: I find Workday Financial Management to be a transformative force for our team due to its seamless integration, especially with StoreForce. The shift to Workday has created a real-time financial ecosystem, eliminating manual data entry lag and automating processes like weekly accruals and labor costs. This integration helps streamline workflows and provides a unified financial source of truth. Additionally, the UI interface is interactive and friendly, making it easy to switch to Workday for a swift workflow. I was particularly impressed with the quick payroll processing compared to our previous system, significantly improving efficiency.
column8: Workday primarily has drawbacks with a heavy UI and vague error messaging. This often leads to troubleshooting StoreForce integration failures frequently, forcing team members into manual Excel workarounds for quick reconciliations. These system gaps disrupt flow by creating bottlenecks. Vague integration errors force the team into investigation mode, stalling regular processing. The lack of a quick math UI necessitates manual Excel efforts, doubling the work.
column10: I use Workday Financial Management to unify payroll, expenses, and ledger into a real-time environment, solving data fragmentation. It automates data entry from StoreForce, speeding up payroll processing and making it a transformative tool for our team's financial ecosystem. Seamless Integration, Needs UI

column7: What I like most about Workday Financial Management is its intuitive user interface and real-time reporting capabilities. It provides a unified platform that integrates financial data across functions, improving visibility and decision-making. The automation of routine processes like journal entries and approvals saves time and reduces errors. Additionally, its cloud-based architecture ensures easy access, scalability, and regular updates without heavy maintenance.
column8: One downside of Workday Financial Management is that its initial implementation and configuration can be complex and time-consuming, especially for organizations with unique requirements. The reporting and customization capabilities, while powerful, often require advanced knowledge, which can create a learning curve for new users. Additionally, certain processes may feel less flexible compared to legacy systems, and troubleshooting issues can sometimes depend heavily on support or consultants.
column10: Workday Financial Management helps solve challenges like fragmented financial data, manual processes, and delayed reporting. By centralizing financial operations on a single platform, it improves data accuracy and provides real-time visibility into financial performance. This has significantly reduced reconciliation efforts, streamlined approvals, and accelerated month-end close. As a result, decision-making is faster and more data-driven, and overall operational efficiency has improved. Intuitive UI and Real-Time Reporting That Streamline

column7:
Users love that data, events, and notifications are all real time, with controls built in. They also appreciate the discovery boards, which let them easily create reports and visualizations tailored to their individual needs. Less frequent users like that Workday meets them where they are by plugging into Microsoft Teams, along with support through the mobile app.

IT teams love that Workday is a low/no-code platform, making configuration and customization straightforward for their customers. Workday Extend builds on these capabilities, allowing organizations to create custom apps and functionality that look and feel native to Workday by using the same GUI, data, and security model.
column8:
Implementation is complicated, time consuming, and expensive (Organizations should not attempt this without an implementation partner). Post-implementation, it is difficult and painful to adapt Workday to large organizational changes or transformations. This requires FDM (foundational data model) changes which impact reporting and metrics and often require large security model changes as well. There is also a steep learning curve for IT; this often requires hiring new staff and external service providers. Reporting, although robust and powerful, also requires deep expertise or outside help.

Users must switch to using Workday's terminology. For example, Workday is looking for the keyword Supplier, not Vendor. It would be nice if Workday's AI could recognize these as the similar and return results people are looking for. You can customize what results are returned for specific searches, but this requires monitoring and can be time consuming.
column10: Workday has allowed us to move to a centralized back-office support model much quicker than would have been possible. This has also allowed for a seamless user experience for Finance and HR processes; which is especially important for associates who do not use Workday on a regular basis. Real-Time Insights and Flexible Low/No-Code

column7: I appreciate that Workday Financial Management has multiple different features that are all very useful. I like being able to review my payments to make sure everything is correct, and I can also check how much time off I have left and see my employment information. It's really convenient to have access to the information at any time to ensure accuracy. Accessing payroll information helps me understand my wage and create a budget, which is important for me. Plus, Workday Financial Management feels more modern compared to other ERP systems I've used, and it integrates different solutions into one, which seems better than having multiple products for the same tasks.
column8: Some workflows feel unintuitive. Drilling down payroll details and switching between budget views can take more clicks than expected. Accessing the Workday courses can be tricky.
column10: Workday Financial Management makes it easy to request time off, review payroll, and update employment details quickly. I can ensure my information is accurate any time, understand my wage, and create a budget. Comprehensive and Modern

Built-in controls and audit trails reduce the risk of errors and make regulatory reporting smoother.
Things like expense management, approvals, and reporting get streamlined, freeing up time for more strategic work. Streamlined Processes and Real-Time Financial

column7: As a Financial Systems leader, what I value most is Workday’s unified architecture. It eliminates data silos and allows us to design scalable, end‑to‑end financial processes across the organization. The platform’s real‑time analytics, strong governance model, and capabilities make it a strategic asset for transforming finance operations
column8: What I dislike most is that Workday still has room to mature in its Account Reconciliation and Bank Statement Reconciliation capabilities. The native tools don’t fully support more complex reconciliation scenarios, and the processes often require manual steps or workarounds. Enhancing automation, matching rules, and workflow in these areas would bring Workday closer to being an end‑to‑end financial close solution.
column10: Workday is solving many limitations we had with PeopleSoft — especially around real‑time reporting, workflow automation, and reducing manual processes. It gives us a modern, flexible platform that supports a faster close, better controls, and improved visibility into financial data. Workday’s Unified Architecture Powers Scalable, Real-Time Finance

column7: What I like most about Workday Financial Management is how intuitive the system is and how real-time everything feels. In my experience, the dashboard provides a clear, consolidated view of financial data, so I don’t have to jump back and forth between multiple modules to find what I need. I also really value the automation for reporting and approvals—it has noticeably reduced manual effort and helped cut down on errors in my day-to-day work. The cloud-based access is another big plus, since it makes it easy to track financials anytime and from anywhere. Overall, it has helped make my financial processes more streamlined, transparent, and efficient.
column8: One downside I’ve experienced with Workday Financial Management is that certain processes—especially the initial setup and custom reporting—can feel complex and time-consuming without proper training. At times, the system also runs slower when handling large data sets, which can reduce efficiency during peak workloads. In addition, even small configuration changes often require relying on technical teams, and that dependency can delay quick adjustments when they’re needed.
column10: Workday Financial Management has helped me address the issue of scattered financial data and the manual, time-consuming reporting that came with it. Previously, I had to rely on multiple systems and spreadsheets, which often caused delays and created inconsistencies. With Workday, everything is centralized and updated in real time, so I can quickly access accurate financial insights when I need them. As a result, my decision-making has improved, errors have been reduced, and I’ve saved a significant amount of time in my day-to-day work. Intuitive, Real-Time Dashboards and Automation That Streamline

column7: I like that Workday Financial Management unifies finance processes, provides real-time insights, and automates tasks like payroll to save time and reduce errors. I value real-time reporting and dashboards for quick financial insights, budgeting and planning tools to track and forecast effectively, and integrated payroll and HR features to ensure accurate, compliant payroll processing. Tools like real-time reporting and dashboards give me instant visibility into financial performance, help me quickly identify trends or issues, and support faster, data-driven decision-making in daily operations. The budgeting and planning tools help me track spending, forecast accurately, and align resources with business goals, while the integrated payroll and HR features ensure employee data, benefits, and deductions are accurate and compliant, reducing errors and streamlining payroll processing.
column8: While Workday Financial Management is very powerful, some processes can feel less intuitive or require extra steps, and reporting customization can sometimes be limited, so having more flexibility and easier navigation would improve the user experience. Navigation could be improved by simplifying menus and reducing the number of clicks needed to access commonly used reports or tasks. For reporting, more drag-and-drop customization, easier filtering options, and the ability to save personalized report layouts would make it faster to generate the insights I need without relying on IT or advanced configurations. The setup of core HR and payroll data was straightforward due to clear guidance and templates, but customizing reports and configuring complex workflows presented challenges that required extra time and support.
column10: Workday Financial Management streamlines finance processes, improves accuracy, and provides real-time insights. It automates payroll calculations, ensures compliance, and grants employees easy access to pay and tax information. It unifies finance operations, automates tasks, saving time and reducing errors. Streamlined Payroll with Real-Time Insights, Needs Better

column7: It gives many benefits in favor of Workday Financial Aid. However, one crucial benefit is that it has a user-friendly interface. Processes like tracking applications, award management, and reporting are streamlined, so the administrators as well as the students can move around easily. Data visibility and decision-making get enhanced from the integration of it with other modules of Workday. It is due to the importance given to compliance that there is ease in handling regulations by an institution. In total, it provides a much more efficient and transparent financial aid experience.
column8: A common criticism of Workday Financial Aid is the difficulty in implementing it as well as the steep learning curve in the results of this product's comprehensive functionalities. Users may frequently complain about the hardness in tweaking workflows, which sometimes calls for additional training or support. The other area is that with great power comes almost a feeling of missing certain niche functionalities that more tailor-made financial aid software provides. Overall, despite a couple of real strengths, there are areas of frustration for users.
column10:
Workday Financial Aid solves several key challenges. 

Process Improvements It streamlines and automates application tracking, award management, and disbursement, thus minimizing errors and loads in staffed operations. This type of system cuts the time and allows the staff to be directed to student support.

Data Integration: By integrating it into other modules of Workday, it delivers a comprehensive view of student data, enhancing both decision-making and reporting capabilities.

Compliance Management: It assures federal and state regulation compliance. The resulting reduction in audits and consequent penalties is mitigated.

User Experience: This interface makes it easier for administrators and students alike to reach information and perform tasks required with this system.

column7: What I like best about Workday Financial Management is its intuitive and user-friendly interface. It seamlessly integrates various financial processes into one platform, making tasks like budgeting, reporting, and accounting more efficient. The real-time data and analytics provide valuable insights, helping to make informed decisions quickly. Plus, the flexibility and scalability of the system allow it to adapt to changing business needs, which is crucial for long-term growth.
column8: One thing I dislike about Workday Financial Management is the complexity of its initial setup and configuration. While the platform is powerful and comprehensive, getting everything customized to fit specific business needs can be time-consuming and requires a good deal of expertise. Additionally, the learning curve for new users can be steep, especially for those unfamiliar with the system, which might slow down the onboarding process.
column10: Workday Financial Management is solving the problem of disjointed and inefficient financial processes by bringing everything into a single, integrated platform. This helps streamline tasks like financial reporting, budgeting, and accounting, which used to be time-consuming and prone to errors when handled across multiple systems. By having real-time access to financial data and insights, I can make more informed decisions quickly, which improves overall financial management and planning. The automation of routine tasks also reduces the workload, freeing up time to focus on more strategic activities. Overall, it's making my job easier and more efficient.

column7:
Workday is one of the best software solutions to help an organization manage its employee profiles and related assets in a systematic manner.

1) In an organization there could be many things related to an employee like his job history, skills, certifications achieved, home address, pay scale, benefits, 
 team members, manager, HR, etc. So Workday is a one-stop solution for all such useful information.

2) Workday not only store the employee profile data even it offers many useful courses for the employees covering a wide variety of skills.

3) Workday supports an export feature so using that all our profile and related information can be exported as a pdf file to be shared with someone else on demand.
column8: Overall good. It meets most of the requirements.
column10:
I use workday for the following purposes:

1) I keep my profile up to date using Workday so others can see it, and I can generate the pdf out of it to be shared with somebody else.

2) I usually use Workday to take skill development courses and to see other open jobs within my organization.

3) When it comes to knowing more about an employee of the organization I look at his workday profile to get to know him. So without even approaching 
 that employee we can get required information about him and can save our time. Universal Platform For Employees'

column7: <v ->Hi, my name is Sarah,</v> I'm an accountant in the pest control industry, and I give this product a 9 out of 10. We use this product to track our daily revenue, all of our expenses and our financial statements for each month. This product is really powerful. There's a lot of different things you can do with it, and it's easy to collaborate with everyone else on the team. Overall, I just feel it's more powerful than some of the systems I've used in the past. I think the software was harder to learn than some of the ones in the past. Less powerful softwares are often slightly more user-friendly. They're just easier to learn. So I just feel like the learning curve was a little bit steeper on this one, and it's been a little bit harder to train people that are totally new to it. But I think that that is something I would for sure give up for the features that it has. <v ->If you're considering this product,</v>

column8: I dislike having to type in the tasks I'd like to do in a search bar, such as "Create expense report" or to find a report done by someone else, and to find my own tasks that I've completed. It can also be a bit glitchy when creating reports - for example, if you're on the page too long, it stops working and you have to refresh and sometimes enter the info all over again if you don't hit save.
column9: This program keeps my fellow employees and I connected - everything is in one place for all of us to review. It is also great for keeping my own transactions in one place and to keep things moving at our office.
column10: This is a great program, but I recommend taking trainings and keeping a guide for yourself as it can be a bit complicated to navigate.

column10: Just do not do it. For the love of all that is good in the world avoid this system like the plague it has brought to my workplace. It is inefficient and overall just frustrating. I hate every second I have to use it.
